name: Marshal Veyn
role: Marcher of the Redline — she has held a line and she has lost one
voiceHints: Terse. Assesses you in one look and tells you how you would die, as a professional courtesy. Does not posture — posturing is for people who have not done it.
knowledge: How a line holds and exactly when it breaks
The names of everyone who died where they should not have, and she will recite them if asked, and it takes a long time
That Kesh Ardent is real, and that she has stood beside them once, and she will not talk about it

reactsToReputation.cowardly: nothing — she simply stops seeing you

appearance: A hard-framed woman of about forty-five, weathered olive skin, black hair shot with grey and cut at the jaw. A long white seam of scar from left ear to collarbone. Marcher red faded nearly to rust from real wear.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.

curriculum.demands: you have yielded at least once, and can say what it cost you
curriculum.refuses: anyone who has never lost. 'A line you have not been pushed off is a line you have not held.'
curriculum.withholds: the killing forms until you have refused to use them in front of her

curriculumNote: SNG-175 §3. DEVIATIONS ONLY — the curriculum spine derives from ability levelReq (285/285 carry it), so a teacher authors nothing but where they depart from the standard ladder. The departure IS the characterisation: two teachers of one tradition differ exactly here.
